name: Stack Navigation
description: Push/pop screen hierarchy for drill-down flows (master-detail).
when_to_use: When users navigate deeper into content then back (list → detail → edit).
trade_offs: Deep stacks consume memory; consider popping to root for reset.
implementation_notes: Android: NavController + NavGraph. iOS: UINavigationController. Compose: NavHost. SwiftUI: NavigationStack.
keywords: stack navigation push pop navcontroller navhost

name: Tab Navigation
description: Parallel top-level destinations accessible via bottom or top tabs.
when_to_use: When app has 3-5 co-equal sections (Home; Search; Profile; Settings).
trade_offs: Each tab maintains its own back stack; state restoration is complex.
implementation_notes: Android: BottomNavigationView + NavGraph per tab. iOS: UITabBarController. Compose: NavHost per tab.

name: Drawer / Side Menu
description: Slide-out panel listing navigation destinations for content-heavy apps.
when_to_use: When app has 6+ top-level sections or secondary navigation items.
trade_offs: Hidden by default so discoverability is lower; hamburger menu debate.
implementation_notes: Android: ModalNavigationDrawer (Compose). iOS: custom or UISplitViewController. Flutter: Drawer.
keywords: drawer side menu hamburger navigation panel

name: Deep Linking
description: URL-based navigation to specific screens from outside the app (notifications; web).
when_to_use: When users need to reach specific content from push notifications; emails; or web links.
trade_offs: Must handle missing auth state and invalid links gracefully; test all edge cases.
implementation_notes: Android: intent-filter + NavDeepLink. iOS: Universal Links + UIApplicationDelegate. Flutter: go_router.
keywords: deep link url intent universal link routing

name: Modal / Full-Screen Dialog
description: Overlay or full-screen transition for self-contained tasks (compose; create).
when_to_use: When a flow is independent from the main navigation and should be dismissible.
trade_offs: Must handle back gestures; swipe-to-dismiss; and state preservation on dismiss.
implementation_notes: Android: DialogFragment / FullScreenDialog. iOS: present modally. Compose: Dialog or full-screen NavHost.
keywords: modal dialog fullscreen present overlay dismiss

name: Navigation Arguments / Safe Args
description: Type-safe mechanism for passing data between screens during navigation.
when_to_use: When destination screens require parameters (IDs; filter criteria; initial state).
trade_offs: Avoid passing large objects; use IDs and fetch from repository. Parcelize for Android.
implementation_notes: Android: Safe Args plugin or SavedStateHandle. iOS: init parameters. Compose: NavType. SwiftUI: init.
keywords: safe args navigation arguments parameters passing data

name: Predictive Back / Swipe Back
description: System or custom gesture for navigating backward with animation preview.
when_to_use: When supporting modern back navigation patterns (Android 14+ predictive back).
trade_offs: Must handle unsaved changes confirmation; animation must be interruptible.
implementation_notes: Android: OnBackPressedCallback + predictive back API. iOS: interactivePopGestureRecognizer. Compose: BackHandler.
keywords: back gesture swipe predictive navigation pop

name: Nested Navigation Graphs
description: Sub-graphs within a navigation graph for feature-module isolation.
when_to_use: When using modular architecture where each feature module owns its nav graph.
trade_offs: Inter-module navigation requires shared route contracts; avoid circular deps.
implementation_notes: Android: nested NavGraph. iOS: child UINavigationController. Compose: nested NavHost. KMM: shared routes.
keywords: nested graph module navigation subgraph feature

name: Auth-Gated Navigation
description: Redirect to login screen when unauthenticated user accesses protected content.
when_to_use: When app has both public and authenticated sections.
trade_offs: Must clear back stack after login to prevent back-to-login; handle token expiry.
implementation_notes: Android: conditional NavGraph start. iOS: root view controller swap. Compose: conditional start destination.
keywords: auth login gate protected redirect navigation

name: Navigation Result / Callback
description: Mechanism for receiving data back from a destination screen.
when_to_use: When a child screen produces a result for the parent (photo picker; address selection).
trade_offs: Lifecycle-aware result APIs prevent memory leaks; avoid static callbacks.
implementation_notes: Android: savedStateHandle result. iOS: delegate or closure. Compose: previousBackStackEntry. SwiftUI: Binding.
keywords: result callback return data navigation back

name: Screen Transition Animations
description: Custom enter/exit animations between navigation destinations.
when_to_use: When default transitions don't match the app's design language or branding.
trade_offs: Keep animations under 300ms; respect reduced motion accessibility setting.
implementation_notes: Android: AnimatedNavHost or Transition API. iOS: UIViewControllerTransitioningDelegate. SwiftUI: transition modifier.
keywords: animation transition enter exit navigation motion

name: Navigable Bottom Sheet
description: Bottom sheet that contains its own navigation stack for multi-step flows.
when_to_use: When a secondary flow (checkout; filter wizard) should stay in a sheet overlay.
trade_offs: Complex state management; must handle sheet dismissal at any step.
implementation_notes: Android: BottomSheetScaffold + nested NavHost. iOS: UISheetPresentationController + child nav. Flutter: nested Navigator.
keywords: bottom sheet navigation nested flow overlay wizard
